items.title: Inquiry into violence against sex workers to examine stubborn stigma and legal solutions

items.title: Once an 'embarrassment' China's EV industry now leads the world
items.summary: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

items.title: 'Narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home, studies show. But are they right?
items.summary: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

items.summary: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

items.title: Lesbian couples considered separate families in updated Queensland Health fertility clinic guidance
items.summary: Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

items.title: Paramedics told to take complex cardiac patients to Launceston General Hospital instead of Mersey
items.summary: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

items.title: Doctors worry health system can't make up for loss of services if Hobart Private sells to Catholic-run Calvary

items.title: ABC Your Say invites people across the country to tell us what you think
